questions.prompt: In a SIP trace, you see REGISTER → 401 → REGISTER (with Auth header) → 401 → REGISTER (with Auth) → 403. What's the most likely cause?
choices: The PBX is rate-limiting the user
The password digest isn't matching; after a few failures the PBX is blocking with 403
questions.explanation: A 401 in response to a REGISTER with an Authorization header means the digest didn't validate. After repeated failures, most PBXs return 403 Forbidden and may auto-block the source IP via security rules. Wrong password is the prime suspect; non-existent extension would return 404, not 401.

questions.prompt: Which SIP response code class indicates the called party-side specifically said no for some reason (not available, not allowed, doesn't exist)?
questions.explanation: 4xx codes are client-side / called-side errors: 404 not found, 480 unavailable, 486 busy, 403 forbidden, etc. 5xx is server / infrastructure failure (e.g. 503 from a sick trunk).
